title: Peter Walker, Head of Insights at Carta - Startups, Funding, Investors, AI & Bay Area

source.description:
Curious about the future of startups?  Join us for an insightful conversation with Peter Walker, Head of Insights at Carta.

We crack the code on:

- Cutting-edge data trends in venture capital
- Mastering equity sharing and SAFEs like a pro
- The secret sauce of the Bay Area startup scene 
- How accelerators can propel your startup
- Networking tips for building connections that matter
- Demystifying startup valuations 
- The power of cross-cultural communication
- Why investing in female founders is critical

Carta - an ownership and equity management platform trusted by thousands of founders, investors, and employees. 

Learn more about Carta: https://carta.com/

Learn more about Carta Launch: https://carta.com/equity-management/launch/

Connect with Peter Walker on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/peterjameswalker/

Connect with Dasha on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/dashashunina/

Connect with Dasha on Instagram https://www.instagram.com/feed_my_unicorn/

Sponsor next episode of Talks With Dasha - dariashunina@yahoo.com, Subject - Podcast Sponsorship. 

Timecodes:

00:00 – Meet Peter Walker, Head of Insights at Carta  
00:19 – What Carta does for startups and founders  
00:49 – At what stage do founders typically reach out to Carta? About Carta Launch, a free cap table software for startup founders  
01:30 – Data is the new oil  
02:30 – It’s going to be a long time before we see the fundraising amounts that we saw in 2021  
03:49 – The typical size of the founding team, depending on the specific vertical  
06:15 – How do you typically share equity between co-founders? There should be a boss!  
09:00 – SAFEs: what are they, and what is the best way to use them?  
10:43 – SAFEs vs. convertible notes  
12:00 – Small teams perform better. Startups are hiring less.  
13:32 – For those who are looking for a job in startups  
16:03 – It’s AI and everything else  
17:12 – Making unsexy industries sexy  
18:30 – Choosing an AI company to invest in  
19:17 – It’s the Bay Area and everything else  
21:51 – What is so magical about the Bay Area?  
22:47 – Why do accelerators require founders to participate in their programs in-person?  
23:38 – YC is trying to reach a global audience  
25:04 – Accelerator programs: should founders apply?  
27:48 – Who you know, or what you know?  
29:05 – Networking tips from Peter Walker  
30:40 – Self-presentation template  
35:30 – Cross-cultural communication  
39:44 – Startup valuations  
42:55 – Only 2% of venture capital go to female founders now. How can we change that?  
46:38 – A few companies with great potential

theme.summary: This transcript is fundamentally focused on business strategy, entrepreneurship, and startup development. The entire conversation covers core business concepts including: startup equity management, cap table management, fundraising strategies (SAFEs vs convertible notes), venture capital landscape analysis, startup valuation dynamics, founding team structures and equity splits, business networking strategies, accelerator programs and their business value, hiring strategies for startups, and investment trends across different sectors. While it touches on data analysis and AI trends, these topics are explored through the lens of business strategy, startup growth, and entrepreneurship. Peter Walker's role at Carta involves providing business insights to startups and founders, and the discussion extensively covers practical business advice on everything from fundraising to team building to networking. This aligns perfectly with the existing business category which already contains extensive content on entrepreneurship, startup strategy, business models, venture capital, and company building frameworks from other successful founders and business leaders.
